reviews.why: True homonym. Both sides name rising hourly wages, but scope differs (DDD: store labor vs EEE: warehouse labor) and mechanism differs (DDD: statutory minimum-wage increases, a policy cause, per evC4 "hourly store wages rose with minimum wage increases"; EEE: market-driven labor scarcity, per evD3 "warehouse hourly wages climbed amid tight labor markets"). The 3-check union test fails on both scope and mechanism, so the occurrences are not the same reusable cause. New names use only evidence words ("store/warehouse hourly wages"), keep state words (rose/climbed) and mechanisms out of the name per R2/R7, and name the level the evidence attributes per R9.
